violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units and slicer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025
Bathrooms were observed without proper handwashing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

Raw animal foods (such as raw egg) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as produce) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC informed to use kitchen 3 bay sink or kitchen dishwasher for sanitization. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

Pinto beans and hollandaise were observed held at 127 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

Various items were observed held between 46-61 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

Working containers of liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
Single service items were observed stored on the floor in the back storage area. Single service items must be store at least 6 inches off the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
Refrigerator located in the front area were not in good repair. PIC took out of service. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -prep units in the kitchen, -food storage containers in the kitchen, -refrigerator racks in the front, -inside cabinet in the front. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025
Unused equipment and milk crates observed in the refuse area. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025
A hole in the floor was observed in the front service area. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of black and red residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher (bar) was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC informed to utilize second dishwasher or 3 bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Hollandaise Sauce and Beans was observed held at (84-94) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated to 165-175. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Prep Unit door gaskets and interior door were observed in poor repair. Gaskets loose/torn and panel missing exposing insulation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
Inspector observed a leak from 3 bay sink faucet. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2023
Inspector observed clutter and unused equipment in dumpster area. PIC informed to clean and maintain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/06/2023
